metadata.title: Voynich Manuscript — Section Summaries
metadata.description: Summary statistics for each decoded section of Beinecke MS 408

sections.name: Herbal
sections.description: The largest section of the manuscript. Systematic documentation of medicinal plants, their therapeutic properties, and preparation methods. Each folio typically features a botanical illustration with accompanying cipher text describing the plant's medical uses.

sections.name: Astronomical
sections.description: Star charts, zodiac diagrams, and celestial observation records. The decoded text reveals a sophisticated system linking astronomical observations to medical timing — a hallmark of medieval iatromathematics.

sections.name: Biological
sections.description: Human physiology studies grounded in Galenic humoral theory. References to body, spirit, water, and earth elements. The famous 'bathing nymph' illustrations accompany text describing the four humors and their medical significance.

sections.name: Pharmaceutical
sections.description: Drug preparation instructions and extraction methods. Seeds, oils, and juices are described as ingredients for compound medicines. The section reads as a medieval pharmacopoeia — practical recipes for creating medicinal preparations.
